entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: A legume crop, Arachis hypogaea, studied for the inheritance of flowering time.

entities.name: DAYS TO FIRST FLOWERING
entities.description: The number of days from seedling emergence to the appearance of the first flower.

entities.name: DIALLEL CROSS
entities.description: A mating design used to evaluate general and specific combining ability among parents and their progenies.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The environmental period during 1981 and 1982 when the evaluations were conducted.

entities.name: ICRISAT CENTRE PATANCHERU INDIA
entities.description: The research facility where the peanut evaluations were performed.

entities.name: GENETIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: The process of analyzing the inheritance and variance of the flowering trait.

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENETIC VARIANCE
entities.description: The component of genetic variance that is due to the additive effects of alleles, influencing trait expression.

entities.name: GENOTYPE 91176
entities.description: A specific peanut genotype showing high general combining ability for early flowering.

entities.name: GENERAL COMBINING ABILITY
entities.description: The average effect of a genotype in a series of crosses.

entities.name: VARIETY M 13
entities.description: A specific peanut variety showing high general combining ability for late flowering.

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Systematic efforts to improve crop traits through selection and crossing.
